channel.title: Posts on ink 🖋️
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on ink 🖋️

item.title: Managing the Greeks is everything in options trading
item.description: Realized that options trading is all about managing all the Greeks, not just delta. Here's why 20 delta short puts with proper Greek management is the way to go.

item.title: Spreading my bets across AI agents
item.description: My current setup of AI agents - OpenCode, Claude, Kilo Code, Roo Code, and the voice models. Let's see what sticks.

item.title: NIFTY Valuation Calculator
item.description: Interactive NIFTY 50 valuation tool. Plug in your assumptions for bond yields, earnings growth, and risk premium to calculate fair P/E ratios and determine if the market is cheap or expensive.

item.title: Nocturnal Animals: When cinema becomes a mirror
item.description: A raw reaction to Tom Ford's Nocturnal Animals and the disturbing truths it reveals about violence, vulnerability, and the darkness that exists in our world.

item.title: The cost of forgetting: missing expiry day gamma moves
item.description: Missing a profitable expiry day trade because I forgot. A reminder that an edge unused is no edge at all.

item.title: From AI to brain, and brain to AI.
item.description:
(description::A draft of the blog post has to be published on Bearblog. )

Currently, both the human brain and AI systems operate largely as “black boxes.” 🧠 Notably, AI training architectures, especially neural networks, were significantly influenced by our understanding of brain function. Modern AI/LLMs are comparable to a child: they learn by replicating speech and text, not through genuine comprehension. This indicates AI is in a nascent state, projected to mature considerably within 20-30 years.

item.title: India's earnings and spending. - Post
item.description:
(description::)

Check how much India is paying in interest and as a percentage of its revenue. Also, determine the trajectory of this over the years.

Additionally, ascertain if our revenue expenditure is less or more than our total revenue or earnings.

item.title: iPhone Battery Saving
item.description:
• Turn off Background App Refresh
  • Turn Auto App Updates in Setting for App Store
  • Turn On Content Blockers in Safari
  • Turn on Auto Brightness and dim the Brightness too.
  • Reduce Motion and other Transitions.
  • Find Battery Killers in Battery in Settings.
  • Deny locations for Individual Apps.
  • Turn Email Push off and Reduce the auto update frequency for emails.
  • Lower Auto Lock timer.
  • Turn Airdrop off.

Source(Archived)

item.title: Quotes
item.description:
label: Create Quote
icon: ""
style: default
class: ""
cssStyle: ""
backgroundImage: ""
tooltip: ""
id: create-quote
hidden: false
actions:
- type: templaterCreateNote
templateFile: Templates/TEMPLATE - Quote.md
folderPath: /
fileName: "QUOTE"
openNote: true
openIfAlreadyExists: false

%% DATAVIEW_PUBLISHER: start

TABLE WITHOUT ID link(file.link,quote) as Quotes WHERE !contains(file.folder, "Template") & contains(file.tags, "quote")

%%

Quotes
“Warren (Buffett) was playing golf at Pebble Beach with Charlie Munger (Berkshire Hathaway Vice-Chairman), Jack Byrne (Fireman’s Fund Chairman), and another person. One of them proposed. “Warren, if you shoot a hole-in-one on this 18-hole course, we’ll give you U$10,000. If you don’t shoot a hole-in-one, you owe us U$10”. Warren thought about it and said, “I’m not taking the bet.” The others said, “Why don’t you? The most you can lose is U$10. You can make U$10,000.” Warren replied, “If you are not disciplined in the little things, you won’t be disciplined in the big things.”
“Finish every day and be done with it. You have done what you could; some blunders and absurdities no doubt crept in; forget them as soon as you can. Tomorrow is a new day; you shall begin it well and serenely, and with too high a spirit to be cumbered with your old nonsense.” — Ralph Waldo Emerson
“Statistics are like lungis. What they reveal is suggestive, what they hide is vital”.
“The goal is not to be consistently right, but to be right enough when it matters.” - Don’t remember where I got this.
“If you don’t bet, you can’t win. If you lose all your chips, you can’t bet.” - Larry Hite
[[20250507155203 QUOTE.md|The best thing about reading is not that it makes you smart — it doesn’t. What it does, though, is give you some perspective and change the way you think.- @Bhuvan]]
“Incentives drive everything, and most of us underestimate what we’d be willing to do if the incentives were right.” - Morgan Housel
Not having FOMO (fear of missing out) is the most important financial skill. It’s crucial for building wealth. If you are susceptible to FOMO, it’s hard to accumulate significant wealth over time. - Morgan Housel
Be fearful when others are greedy & greedy when others are fearful - Warren Buffett
  • As for history, we are living in its ruins. And as for biographies, we are living with the consequences of all the decisions ever made in them. I tend not to read them for pleasure. It’s not unlike carefully scrutinizing the map when one has already reached the destination.
  • “You can get a good deal from rehearsal, If it just has the proper dispersal. You would just be an ass, To do it en masse, Your remembering would turn out much worsal.” Ulrich Neisser
  • Admiration is a state furthest away from understanding. - Ruth Bader Ginsburg
  • Don’t be distracted by emotions like anger, envy, resentment. This just zap energy and time. - Ruth Bader Ginsburg
  • You can’t have it all, at once. - Ruth Bader Ginsburg
  • So that’s the dissenter’s hope; that they are writing not for today, but for tomorrow. - Ruth Bader Ginsburg

%% DATAVIEW_PUBLISHER: end %%

item.title: Watch Low Cost or Free Anime, TV Shows or Movies.
Indian market has a long runway to grow.
item.description:
Indian Indices, don’t have companies that belong to

  • Education
  • Restaurant
  • Furniture
  • Sports
  • Gaming
  • Payment Solutions

TODO

  • Comb through all Major Indices and see which type of company we don’t here.

item.title: Black mirror: Bandersnatch - the interactive nightmare that broke my brain
item.description: My deep dive into Black Mirror: Bandersnatch - the interactive movie that left me questioning free will, simulation theory, and what it means to be in control

item.title: Fractured (2019) - The Psychological Thriller That Gaslit Me
item.description: How Fractured uses unreliable narration and creeping dread to create one of the most unsettling psychological thrillers I've seen

item.title: The Others (2001) - The Horror Movie That Flipped Everything
item.description: Why The Others is the most underrated horror masterpiece - a gothic thriller that redefined the ghost story with one of cinema's greatest twists

item.title: Mind-blowing video about the way we see the world
Why, I think, I lost my empathy？
item.description:
Fear of failure.

Fear of being incompetent.

Fear of Future Uncertainty. Keeps my brain always occupied, thinking of ways to mitigate it. Does not leave me, with enough bandwidth, to feel empathy for others.

item.title: Intentions don't matter, actions do, in short term.
item.description:
In interview, don’t think about being real, just fake enough to pass through.

Don’t try to be a good guy, just be good enough, day to day.

item.title: Be Bored.
item.description: A post about benefits of being bored.
